reviews.review_text: Imagine if This War of Mine was actually about zombies. That's the best way to describe this game.

pure gold, they keep updating the game faster then i can play.

Devs your doing an amazing job in early access just get voice actors for the survivors!

[h1] The City's Dead So It's Time To Leave [/h1]

[b] The Positive's [/b]
+ Great Map Designs
+Multiple Endings
+ Fun Mechanics

[b] The Negative's Did Not Survive... [/b]

[b] The Aspect Of The Game [/b]
[i] You and another are remnants of a city dying to a plague of the undead. Move from base to base searching place to place for a way out before the city takes you too... [/i]

[b] My Two Cent Review [/b]
[i] It's tough nowadays to make an exciting zombie game because the genre is over saturated with games, movies, and comic books you've seen everything and yet this game is refreshing to me to play. It's almost if This War Of Mine & State Of Decay had a love child together and this is the end result. It may not be military war torn city but it is a zombie torn city where it's the massive amounts of undead versus the very few living left alive. The survival mechanic and time phases are great editions to the game along with random events that play out as time goes by. There's food spoilage mechanic so you have plan out accordingly however it would be a cool edition if we could make something in the future to preserve food longer. All of your weapons except firearms have durability's to them so after a couple of sneaky zombie executions or going at them with a weapon for a few swings it will break and pretty fast so that's another thing you have to account for which is that your weapons are flimsy as can be so you better bring back ups when your first weapon breaks clearing out a location and it will break. There are multiple endings to the game I probably got the worst one trusting the wrong people. There is replayability to this game and I will going back to play it again once the full game has released as of the time writing this review the game is half way finished I'm just happy they actually put endings in this early access and I'm excited to see what the full release brings. [/i]

[b] The Value Of The Game [/b]
[i] 25$ dollars at the time of writing this is a fair price for what they have out so far and for what they plan to add. If they keep working on it and adding more features that don't backfire or stir up controversy because most developers do for some reason I can see the price even going up to 30$! If you like zombie survival games this is one of the best ones  I have played. [/i]

[h1] Final Score 95/100 Ranking A Tier [/h1]

Lotta fun!  Graphics look great.  Strong "This War of Mine" vibes.  Using the xbox controller.  

My only complaint is Perma-Iron man mode, b/c Im a world-class Save Scummer so Ive restarted a bunch just trying to get going and dying due to zombies and gravity, etc.  So I'd probably have triple the hours played if I didn't throw up my hands in frustration because I can't just take the loss and press on with who I have left.  

Great game overall!

I like the game, but it can be incredibly frustrating and inventory management feels tedious and overly restrictive.  I do recommend it, but I also plan to quit playing for a while and maybe return after a few updates.  Here are a few gripes I hope they address...

Melee Weapon Durability is very frustrating.  Even weapons with high durability often won't last through a single exploration mission.  Because of this your weapon will break and you will either need to hope you find one or bring extra which takes up more space in your inventory.

Guns and Ammo use individual inventory space, even when the bullets are loaded into the gun.  So, say you bring a rifle with a 20 round magazine.  If you bring 20 rounds your inventory will show a rifle and a pile of ammunition but you don't have any extra ammunition to reload.  Oh, the max stack size for ammo is also 20 so any reloads for that rifle would be an entirely new inventory slot.  The M16 style rifle fires full auto and with a quick click of the mouse you'll fire off a 3 round burst.  Ammo goes pretty quick.

On some exploration trips you will encounter doors or containers that require either a lockpick or bolt cutters to open.

So let's put all of that in our inventory.  You start with 10 slots.  Add an melee weapon, a backup melee weapon, a rifle, two "magazines" (40 rounds), a lock pick, a bolt cutter.  You've used 7 of your 10 available slots.  You can pick up 3 items on your trip, more if you use up the resources you brought with you.  Don't find a lock to pick?  Well, you lose that space.  Skip bringing the lockpick?  Guaranteed to find something that needs one. (feels that way anyway)  This example is a little exaggerated of course, but in my experience I found myself skipping the lock picks and bolt cutters every first visit to a location and only packing them on a return trip if i encounter them.  It kind of sucks that each location pretty much requires multiple trips to clear because each trip equates to a day or night cycle which burns resources.

Deep wounds.  The first time one of my survivors got deep wounds I held out hope that he would not turn.  I treated his wounds.  I treated his fever.  I applied med packs.  After a few days of this he turned anyway.  The second time one of my survivors got deep wounds I held out hope that she would not turn.  I treated her wounds. I treated her fever.  I applied med packs.  After a few days of this she turned anyway.  The third time one of my survivors got deep wounds I held out hope that he would not turn.  I treated his wounds.  I treated his fever.  I applied med packs.  After a few days of this he turned anyway.  Fool me thrice shame on you.  Fool me four times...you won't fool me four times.

Recruiting survivors.  When you meet a survivor they only show you the current traits for that survivor.  You can't see their progression so it is hard to tell if they will be useful or not.  As the game is early access information isn't very good yet on what skills each acquire.  If you accept a survivor and don't like their skill set your morale will suffer if/when you kick them out.

Minor gripe.  Throwables and guns are both used via right mouse click meaning you can only equip one at a time.  You can't, for example, equip a grenade and a rifle.

This game is pure art.

I rarely seen any game with insanly good in art, character design, lighting. the amount of detail in each location, it's not just a reuse location over and over but every location is unique, like big school, small bar, fire houese  etc, it has many and you will be impress.

for gameplay, what else you can say ? this is up there with those legend zombie game, the sound design, many survivor to recruit. it's really good survival zombie game

I just love it, it's that good. you will be impress and appreciate the art of this game.

Its like This War of Mine 2.0, with zombies. If you liked that game, you'll enjoy this one. 

So far my one big gripe is that the <stealth kill> button and the <interact with environment> button are the same button. This has lead to SEVERAL situations where I'm trying to stealth kill a zombie before it turns back around and instead my character is searching for loot because the zombie happened to stop walking right in front of a loot POI.

Having beaten the game now I guess I'd like to add that I wish there was a BIT more text for each survivor's epilogue. And while I had 3 survivors at the end, it only gave me a little blurb on 2 of them; it told me nothing about Mei even though it was her, Hao and Michelle that got out.

Started game optimistic about survival, 
raided some houses, 
found some loot, 
found creepy red room, 
character had nightmares, 
I had nightmares,
moved base, 
found other survivors, 
needed more food, 
went out, 
favourite character died, 
genuinely upset at death, 
felt awful for the now fatherless daughter having to now put up with sassy Barb and some other rando, 
got stuck in a need food/ found little/ starving/ just surviving loop, 
found garden, 
planted veg, 
got more food, 
survivors happy,
me happy, 
good game.

Really good, only thing lacking is in my opinion more character to character interactions, something that makes the shelter feel alive and not a grind fest needed to go to another place loot, but apart from that, great gameplay, great visuals, soundtrack is great, story is great, and the price is 100% worth it. Give this game your money, its honestly really well done, not just for its price, can't wait for future updates.

Literally just This War Of Mine with Zombies, Can't go wrong.

Really well made art style and animations, good gameplay loop and interesting premise without being a rip-off or Cliche, runs beautifully and with constant updates from the developer? It scratches that itch!

Good gameplay mechanics. Really like the locations and story missions for characters + main escape goals. Bad thing that almost everything is on ONE button sometimes getting ♥♥♥♥♥♥. For example in the dispatch room in police station I wanted to stealth kill the zombie next to a radio but opened dialog instead. Well I got ♥♥♥♥♥♥ real good. Fighting more zombies head on can be sometimes weird. But overall I am really enjoying the game.

Into the Dead: Our Darkest Days is one of the most immersive zombie survival games I've played in a while. The atmosphere is tense, the visuals are impressive, and every decision feels meaningful. Managing resources, searching for supplies, and keeping survivors alive creates constant pressure without feeling unfair. The game does a great job of making you care about your group and think carefully about every risk you take. Despite being in Early Access, the foundation is already very strong, and I'm excited to see how it evolves with future updates. If you enjoy survival games with strategy, tension, and a great post-apocalyptic atmosphere, this is absolutely worth playing.
my type of game, similar to state of decay with roguelike group building mechanics, my niche idk if it'll be yours you know, but i love it and i wish there were more games like it

Finally bought this game after being in my Wishlist for almost a year. and so far I've enjoyed it despite being early access. The game has the same feel of "This War of Mine" without the timer for each day giving you leeway and time to manage everything. The gameplay is just as what i saw in the trailer and more. and with each new updates i hope they improve on it more.

Now here's just one CON i have of this game.
The Communal Area - where you have to place 1 selected character at a time is most tedious and with long gameplays i find that feature annoying to deal with instead of just selecting all the character you want in one go. Just like This War of Mine.

I feel like the more people you have, the more managing you have to do if this stays the same.

The Sandbox setting - i wanted the barrier to be immortal removing that part and just focus on my scavenging. i thought the settings where i remove the barricade damage would basically make my base safe from attacks, but for some reason i still get barricades still get damaged and players injured despite putting all the settings to 0. i wonder if thats a glitch or intentional because its very misleading.

-----------------------------

All in all the game is amazing and hoping for more updates.

This war of mine with zombies, just such a good looking and good feeling game to play. It's been such a long time I've actually been scared of zombies and each update just keeps adding more depth to the gameplay like the skills trees all survivors have right now and later this month they're going to add traders. These devs actually listen to the community and even better they keep to a consistent schedule and update frequently. Can't wait to see how this game looks when it's out of early access.

The comparisons to war of mine as obvious, but it's a good thing. This extends the genre and it's a natural fit for zombies. The game feels high quality, polished and I love the hand craft curated locations. Nothing feels tedious or like a place you've been to already. All feel unique. The game feels 80% complete. No game stopping bugs yet. Recommended!
Its good so far. Like 'This War of Mine" but with zombies while having different stories but similar mechanics. I'm still playing it before its full release if that's saying anything.
If you liked fallout shelter or this war of mine, you'll definitely love this. I'll definitely come back after the game releases 1.0 version

Ive only played for 5 hours at the normal difficulty but I have been having a blast with this game. I was skeptikal about the side scroller aspect but I have found it to be fun and unique. This is a great survival horror base managment game. It almost feel like state of decay but 2D.
love this game. simple and effective. the atmosphere is perfect. among the many funny bugs the only one that bothers me is the fire pole in station 8. Looking forward to see what's next, thanks

Wow this game is so much fun. If you enjoy zombie stuff, stealth, sidescrolling gameplay, survival crafting, team and base management, and some roguelike elements, this game is for you! As a lot of other reviews have said, this is similar to This War of Mine. I haven't played that game, but as a newcomer to this style of game, I really enjoy it. 

Some odd FPS drops and stutters here and there. In particular with areas that have a lot of lighting, especially nighttime raids or  "missions". Not much story overall nor for characters unfortunately.

Just keep in mind, this game's focus is stealth, base, and character management. If these aren't your style this may not be for you. Updates seem to occur fairly often for this early access and I'm excited for its full release when it comes around.

*in my Bonnie from Family Guy voice* this game is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good Lois. 

If you love a story driven apocalypse game with fun base building and team management mechanics I highly recommend. The combat is also very fun. I am not usually a big fan of side scrollers but this game is such a refreshing take on the 'zombie game' genre. I am not one to leave reviews usually but I had to because I finally finished my first playthrough and I loved the ending so much. The survivor missions add so much depth and I'm really excited to see the development continue.
a fun survival management game, and the updates are good
I have only played this game on steam deck and it is amazing for a handheld game. runs perfectly. Unsure as to why it is not "verified"

This war of mine gameplay in a zombie apocalypse. It is important to note that the theme of this war of mine does not apply here, but it plays very similar, with a looting and crafting loop, always having you at the edge of your seat scrounging for food mostly.

Its great, the characters included so far are diverse and are all well designed. Melee fighting is a little bit clunky, especially the dodging mechanic, but you get used to it.
I loved this game when I first played it during the free beta sample period. It's only gotten better with the improvements since then.
Классная игра в стиле This War of Mine, с неплохой реиграбельностью.

As someone who enjoys zombie games, I put this slightly below Project Zomboid. Zombie enjoyers are bound to enjoy the game, especially if they enjoy being stealthy, managing resources, and managing the survivors.

The game combines This War of Mine and zombies, which is certainly a lot of fun, especially since it works a lot. 

While the survivors being static on where they are leaves a lot to be desired, the fact that their loot shared and conditions are dynamic is a perfect balancer... kinda... imagine this: you got Joe the Goat, honestly one of the best survivors to recruit early on, and he had 3 (or was it 4? I don't remember) PIPE BOMBS with him.

His loot? Very broken. But his condition balanced it out because he had a fractured wrist, which meant that I had to rush getting the med bay out if I wanted to put him to work dismantling, not to mention that he also had an upset stomach so I had to prioritize grabbing some medicine if I want him eating good.

I also got Hudson in one of my runs who became a drunkard way before I recruited him, which gave him withdrawals if he didn't get his fix of alcohol, which REALLY put a big strain on his morale. I was forced to dedicate him staying in the communal center for around 10 phases in order to keep his morale from going full red.

Those 2 are just examples from 2 of my runs. You can get a survivor who has a lot of good loot and in good condition, making them available for use the moment you get back to your shelter... or you can get the worst thing happen in which the survivor has no good loot and is in dire need of help because they have: an upset stomach, a wrist fracture, bleeding, starving, and a flesh wound.

Trust me, you're in for a panic if you get a good survivor but they show up with those things, especially if you're still at the beginning of your run.

One thing I would like to recommend are some balances on some aspects such as romance. It is just... broken, in my opinion. Put two survivors together (make sure their age gap is not too wide), make them fall in love, and they get an increase in morale, which is great, but its kinda unbalanced in a way that the only way to make the romance a bad move is if the other one dies.

I don't mind it, because love does make a difference in morale, but I just thought its broken because morale, especially in the late game, is pretty damn important.

Another thing I want is to put a suggestion in adding an on and off button on "overcrowding morale hit" in custom difficulties. While a larger group does mean a lot more mouth to feed, I feel that there should be a "stronger together" trait going on. 

I think that's all... also, don't nerf Bowman if that thought ever crossed the devs' minds. His entire thing makes sense since he's a Sergeant AND he has survived countless combat zones.
Me ha gustado el juego tiene una buena dificultad, y tienes que pensar con cuidado, sino te podria salir caro!

This game is insanely good, firm and stable for an Early Access.

I bought it without expectations just to try it out and i was surprised and it surpassed my expectatives. Got addicted to it as soon as I started playing and even played it for an entire night (lol). Despite a few not disruptive bugs, It feels like an already finished game:

- Very well game design, addictive loop. I was hooked since minute 1 and always played it on the hardest difficulty which was truly hard and challenging (which I enjoy a lot).

- When playing it I felt a very range of very intense emotions in a short time depending on the situation I was facing (either at the base or while looting), raging between anxiety, surprise, fear, challenge, focus. Games that makes you feel all of that as a player are not so common to find nowadays so this is a 10/10.

- Very cool graphic style, environments and sound.

- Very cool random events that can be positive or negative. Mysteries that you have to undercover as well related to quests, and decisions that you have to make.

- Intense graphic and sound design in shocking and traumatic scenes that you can find around when looting and feel real powerful and affect your character as well.

- Lots of hours of gameplay with replayability possibilities. I think it also has different paths/quests to take to go to the ending and different endings as well.

- I love that you can find different npc survivors and recruit them to your base, which means it has the drawback of having more mouths to feed so it is harder, but they have their own skills and some of them they have their own quests to complete.

- I love that the initial characters that you start with come in pairs, they have their own personal quests and backstory as well.

- Some of the survivors you find around also has their own skills, personal quests that you can do, and background.

- All of the characters are very interesting, have their own well defined personality, strengths and weakenesses.

- All of the characters can be levelled up and they have (i think 2) different paths for doing so, they are simple to understand still.


TLDR: Very good game, one of the best Early Accesses that I ever played, feels like a finished product already, lots of gameplay hours, good and simple base management and very entertaining fighting and looting as well. Interesting stories, characters, and quests. Little to zero non game-breaker bugs. 

Recommended 10/10

every update there is something new and worth loading back up for, ive had this game for a year or smth and have yet to get bored or dislike it. oh and maybe sometimes it has stability issues but i feel like the updates they roll out, do fix them. i dont remember having to deal with any issues the last few times i played

I needed a fix to fill the void of Project Zomboid (Constant Updates). This game fulfills it, and I'm glad I purchased it. It's grindy, stealthy, and I've had a few moments where my palms were sweaty. I recommend you give the game a chance.

Game is still in early access as I make this review, just before they introduce traders, but so far it's giving a LOT of the same energy that This War of Mine gives and that's a MASSIVE compliment. I've wanted a side-scrolling, base building, "turn based" survival game like TWoM for years and it finally feels like I have it.

I've gotten to the point where I think I can just beat one of the endings and I absolutely see myself playing again.

The random scripted events are definitely welcome to shake things up in positive or negative ways and I'm looking forward to getting more character quests getting implemented. One thing I thought was really interesting is that unlike TWoM, you can basically recruit everyone in a single run. Feeding them? That's your problem if you want it. I personally recruited every single person I came across except one, but only because I personally didn't like his attitude.

Resources are both plentiful and scarce at the same time. There's so much to get out there, but fact of the matter is food spoils and not all bases are built equally. You might pick every location clean, but a lack of water collection capabilities might be the end of you if you don't move. Or growing vegetables. Or selecting who gets what resources.

There's a lot of moving parts and I can't wait for the game to get fully released.

Interesting survival horror zombie game in 2.5D. Some parts are still buggy when interacting with enemies.
There are not a lot of kinds of enemies at the moment and it is not clear how to kill the strongest ones.
I'd like to see more of the survival features which where introduced in the latest hotfix and I really liked them. So I hope they keep up this concepts.

I decided to bite the bullet and spend 20 gbp on this after another player in their review explained how they knew the name of the developer back from iOS apps in their teenage years and it was a pleasure to connect the dots. Though, this game is obviously a lot more fleshed out than running and gunning in a field. 

I've really enjoyed this game so far. Especially the resource management mechanics (which in feel depth but never as grindy as The Alters), sneak (flashlight into the next room to show whether there are any zombies there is a great touch) and combat (sneak attacks and standard button mashing). The game also includes the perfect amount of story and character writing, where there's enough to give the characters personality and quests to form narrative points, but where the story doesn't take front and centre over survival, just compliments it. Stylistically, the font and sound design is great, I thought the nightmare room was an especially cool addition. 

In terms of the negatives, I have encountered a frustrating bug (maybe just a skill issue) on one of the maps there's a zombie next to a radio with a dialogue trigger, when going to sneak attack the zombie it triggered the dialogue and the zombie killed the player whilst I was button mashing trying to get through the dialogue, resulting in a 2 for 1: both a dead survivor and dead lead to another survivor. In the tutorial I'd also appreciate some kind of UI prompt about how it's possible to sprint/jump from tall buildings and take fall damage. I wasn't aware of this when rushed by a rival survivor camp and swandived off a tv repair shop into past tense. 

Overall though, really enjoying this game. Lets see how it goes after another 6 hours!  

Cheers

Really enjoyed the time I've played this game. The environmental story-telling is extremely well done through notes, random events and points of interest. Would definitely give it a try if you're on the fence!

This game giving me the love of the feeling when I played This War of Mine but in the element that I liked. which is zombie and survival.

I feel like there are a lot of potential to be adding more things in the game but in the current state, it is not bad. Still fun to play.

But one thing I would want to suggest and that is the morale. The morale loss for having too many survivors need to be adjust a bit. I mean not everybody would hate to have a lot of company, right? but not in this game. everyone in the shelter hates having a lot of people. So, what I would want to suggest is that it should be different for each survivors. Some might boost their morale but some others are opposite.

Love the art direction. Gameplay loop is alright. Then amount of narrative variation is pretty thoughtful.

Only thing I would change would be mixing and matching my own starting team.

Amazing game so far. Played for a bit when it first came to early access and it was cool, but lacked a lot of QoL features. Now, with all the updates is the perfect time to jump in. Feels like a finished game at this point, whatever else they add will be icing on the cake. 

As of right now they have survivalist skill trees, instant moving to whatever crafting station you want (this is actually huge), and the best, custom difficulty sliders. This is my absolute favorite and what convinced me to dive back in for many playthroughs. I love that you can turn down how much hunger everyone loses each phase, as it seems more realistic to me. You can tweak pretty much everything to have the apocalypse you want to.

Def recommend!

Tense & addicting.

I see other reviews contextualize Into the Dead to this war of mine, but I never played that. Instead I played state of decay 2 and heard this game is similar with world exploration, resource/base management and of course permadeath.

Initially the 2D style put me off being used to 3D roaming, but the game does a great job immersing you in the environment with detailed backgrounds showing a city in ruins and a crime scene in most rooms. While SOD2's unexpected moments come from getting to and from places, ITD's are searching the places themselves, which is the meat of the game.

The locations you scout have a lot of variety that keeps it interesting, some places are small straightforward outlet stores where you're in and out, and some are huge office buildings or hospitals or schools where it feels like a large maze you can get swallowed into. 
I really like the effect of the zombies waiting in the background to hear a loud noise, because in those large places the tension to remain stealthy really feels cranked up lest you're in the middle of a huge building and everything around you wakes up.

What I really like about this game is how tense it feels, like I'm on edge every place I go. I haven't played enough to memorize every location by now, but there are unexpected things like walkways breaking dropping you into a horde, car alarms going off, and discovering nightmare rooms which traumatize your character if they stick around too long.

ITD creates such an intense atmosphere (on normal) that sometimes I have to walk away for a bit but always end up coming back for more. I like how the zombie threat is so present that there's not really a need for special zombies (unless you count armored zombies) to give more of a challenge like other zombie games. 

I'll probably get familiar enough with this game at some point where it'll lose it's uneasy edge, but until that point I'm about 5 gameplays in and still haven't escaped yet on normal (almost did once). The journey trying to reach an escape is addictive and always different each playthrough. For this game being a lower budget horror game, it is really good at what it offers, and I like that the devs have a roadmap and have been consistent with updates adding to the game.

If you like zombie horror games definitely would give this one a recommend 👍.

When I first played this game I was ambivalent about it but now it's one of my top 10 favorites. It is one of the very few Early Access games I keep coming back to, and it's because they put out content updates that make the actual gameplay fresh and interesting with each release. More games should do this.

Fantastic resource management/survival horror game with a lot of different story routes to explore. If you are a fan of This War of Mine, this game feels like it improves upon the gameplay in a variety of ways. There is little down time and character interaction is key. Moving around the map feels great and diversifies each playthrough nicely. Some reviews on here are inaccurate given certain gameplay loops ie: it is absolutely beneficial and manageable to have lots of survivors at your base. In one playthrough I had 8 at once and all were fed well, healed, and happy-- you just have to be good at managing resources and utilizing character perks (ie having counselors in the common area raising the mood of the other survivors).

Overall a real A+ game at a great price that has great style and can be replayed over and over again.
Love the gameplay, feel for my survivors whenever I can't help them. Excited to see what more is brought into the game at 1.0

The gameplay is absolutely fantastic. I CANNOT put this game down. The level designers really show off their skills and every expedition is memorable. Assigning tasks in the base as well is super satisfying and scratches that organizational itch I have in games. Hard to believe this game is in early access. The devs have something special here. Apart from some lagging running up or downstairs, you should have no performance issues.

Nearly a 100% copy-paste of This War of Mine. 
Which is not bad after all since it adds a very good looking 1980's zombie apocalipse vibe to it. 

The game clearly needs to be more polished both gameplay and story-wise. 
Looking forward to see the updates and then I will play more.
For now I just supported the devs with this purchase.
Game receives constant updates, its way different from the original demo and different from my previous playthrough a few months ago. Keeps you coming back every few weeks to see what has changed.
A very enjoyable zombie survival sim in the style of This War of Mine. The amount of frustrating deaths I've suffered is high, but so is morale.

I have been playing this for the last roughly 3 weeks after a couple months off and I must say, PikPok has a wonderful enjoyable experience on their hands. All of the EA updates have felt intentional and thought out. Nothing seems accidental.

Huge props to the audio team. The sound effects and ambience set the tone so well, and the number of times I've gotten myself killed simply by not paying attention to the audio is.. well more than zero.

I love the characters, the dialogue, the whole experience. Very enjoyable, pick this up.

Game is good, mechanics are good, I'm ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ WASHED at the combat, I have a major skill issue. (Sorry darrell, leo, penny or whatever her name was, and everyone else I got killed with my dookie butt combat)
its like this war of mine but with zombies and they are very deadly if you dont plan your actions right the stories are nice too
I like the overall graphics as well as the storyline. Resembles to This War of Mine but with zombies!!
Idk for sure, but all of the artwork looks like pure generative Ai slop. There's nothing really happening as far as gameplay either. I hate when a new IP emerges just to be sloppified and remarketed.

A mobile game I completed last year and now reviewing. Not pc compatible just a poorly designed and poorly executed attempt at making a mobile game for pc. 

Cheap and boring.
This game scratches the zombie game itch in my brain. Very happy I got this game.
Great game, Ive been playing Hard for some time and im hating it 10/10
This is such a great game. Especially on the steam deck.
Great game already with updates it keeps getting better!
One of the best arcade style zombie survival games I played. Multi ending stories that you can play again and again.
Very good game. I have played it only little. It is the better version of this war of mine with many new features and story is good too.

(actually, around 20 hours played)

This is simply an improved version of This War of Mine, but in a different setting and with its own atmosphere - without the soppiness and pretensions to seriousness. The main plus is that they removed that boring, stuffy base management which in TWoM just stretched out the time. Here everything is clear and fast: click a couple of times, distribute resources, queue up items, and off you go - into the raid. The base is only there to pull yourself together, rest from the action, and gear up for the next run.

I play on Hard - that's where the real thrill is. Weapons and resources are scarce. Zombies more often roam in groups of 2-3, and "sleeping" ones get up more frequently, so a stealth kill from behind isn't always possible without consequences.

The sight of even a single zombie is frightening, let alone two. You fully immerse yourself in the atmosphere - as if you're really there. You attack from behind cautiously, and when you see a pair, it's a shock because there's no way around them. If the fight doesn't go as planned, you immediately flee the location. If you have no weapon and see a stack of zombies, you don't even enter that location - you just turn around and leave.

The game keeps you tense: you REALLY fear the zombies. On Normal difficulty, it's just another fighting game - no atmosphere, no fear, no tension. But on Hard - it's true survival.

And yes, the developers aren't standing still. They've heavily reworked the difficulty (what was Hard six months ago doesn't compare to the current Hard). They fixed off-screen zombies, added diverse starting card sets, and recently rebalanced the heroes.

P.S. Btw, expecting realism is weird In this setting. TWoM, with its pretension to seriousness, is actually full of "childish" moral dilemmas and conventions that wouldn't exist in real survival. This game doesn't even pretend to be realistic - it's just cool, atmospheric, and honest in its Hard mode. Strongly recommended.
